prs.title: [Core][WIP] Check for GPU<->CPU sync during CI
prs.author: njhill
prs.body_head:
vLLM now uses asynchronous scheduling by default and in the majority of cases. Performance relies on the absence of any gpu<->cpu synchronizations on the main cuda stream, but such syncs can be opaque and it is easy for them to creep in accidentally.

This change adds a `VLLM_GPU_SYNC_CHECK` env var which enables `torch.cuda.set_sync_debug_mode` for the model forward pass and sampler, so that we can easily check for such syncs.

I'm trying first to enable it globally in the CI to flush out syncs that need to be fixed or where they are unavoidable and the check needs to be suppressed. Will then probably split the fixes into separate PR(s).

prs.title: [ROCm] Add env flags to disable dynamic MXFP4 quant and enable AITER tuned GEMMs for Attention Projection Layers
prs.author: heachary
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
For models in the DeepSeek family, dynamic MXFP4 quantization is enabled by default for the attention projection layers. This behavior may be suboptimal in certain deployment scenarios.

This PR introduces two opt-in environment variables that provide finer control over this path:

1. The ability to disable dynamic MXFP4 quantization for attention projections.
2. The ability to route unquantized attention GEMMs through AITER's newer FlyDSL-based tuned GEMM implementations, instead of the default skinny-reduce or hipBLASLt paths.

In internal benchmarks, using the FlyDSL GEMM path for unquantized attention projections shows a good performance improvement on models such as Kimi-K2-Thinking.

## Summary
This PR adds two new ROCm environment variables to give operators finer control over quantization and GEMM dispatch for attention linear layers:
- **`VLLM_ROCM_DISABLE_ATTENTION_LINEAR_LAYER_DYNAMIC_MXFP4_QUANT`** (`False` by default) — When enabled, skips the dynamic MXFP4 quantization that is applied to attention projection layers (`q_proj`, `k_proj`, `v_proj`, `o_proj`) which is done upon model load for a certain family of models.
- **`VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_TUNED_UNQUANTISED_GEMM`** (`False` by default) — When enabled, routes unquantized GEMMs in the attention path through AITER's tuned GEMM implementation (`aiter.tuned_gemm.tgemm.mm`) instead of the default skinny-reduce or hipBLASLt paths.

Both flags are opt-in and default to the current behavior, so there is no change for existing users.

## Test Result
### Accuracy
Model: Kimi-K2-Thinking-MXFP4, TP=4, MI355X, fp8 KV cache

Benchmark | Metric | Score | Threshold | Status
-- | -- | -- | -- | --
GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (flexible-extract) | 0.944 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS
GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (strict-match) | 0.936 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS

### Performance
Model: Kimi-K2-Thinking-MXFP4, TP=4, MI355X, fp8 KV cache

| config | conc | Baseline | AiterTunedFI |

prs.title: [Quantization] quantized kv cache loading for models like qwen
prs.author: xuebwang-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose This PR aims to fix quantized kv cache model loading issues using AMD-Quark. The fix was initially applied to the `gpt-oss` model and may be extended to other models in `vllm/model_executor/models`, as they share a nearly identical methodology. Here is the original error traceback w/o this PR fixing. ## Here is a check list of some models defined in `vllm/model_executor/models/` for the supportness of quantized kv cache loading. Models with `get_cache_scale` called is marked as ✅, while models without `get_cache_scale` called is marked as ⚠️. The ⚠️ symbol is preferred over ❌ since some models can intrinsically have non-quantized KV cache, for instance: - VL models - MLA models - _etc_ Partial list is: ⚠️baichuan ⚠️bloom ✅ dbrx ⚠️ deepseek ⚠️falcon ⚠️ gemma ✅ gemma2 ✅ gemma3 ✅ gpt_j ⚠️ gpt_neox ⚠️ **_gpt_oss_**: this PR initially aims for ✅ grok1 ✅ hunyuan_v1 ✅ llama ✅ llama4 ✅ mixtral ⚠️ mixtral ✅ phimoe ⚠️ qwen ✅ qwen2 ⚠️ qwen2_moe ⚠️ qwen3_moe ... ## Test Plan ## Test Result ---
Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist - [ ] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)". - [ ] The test plan, such as providing test command. - [ ] The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results - [ ] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model. - [ ] (Optional) Release notes update. If your change is user facing, please update the release notes draft in the [Google Doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YyVqrgX4gHTtrstbq8oWUImOyPCKSGnJ7xtTpmXzlRs/edit?tab=t.0).
